options: The pilot flying the drone by looking at it directly with the naked eye
The drone flying fully autonomously, without a pilot
The pilot only looking at the camera feed through goggles
Flying the drone at night with lights
questions.explanation: Line-of-sight (LOS) means the pilot looks up at the sky and flies the drone while seeing it with the naked eye. FPV, by contrast, means looking through a camera.

questions.prompt: Why is FPV flight not considered visual line-of-sight (VLOS)?
options: Because FPV drones are very fast
Because the pilot sees the drone through a camera/goggles, not with the naked eye
Because FPV is only done indoors
Because VLOS only applies to airplanes
questions.explanation: VLOS is the pilot's direct visual contact with the aircraft without any aids. In FPV you look at the aircraft through a camera, so it does not count as visual line-of-sight.

questions.prompt: What is the main job of a spotter during an FPV flight?
options: Charging the drone's battery
Operating the radio on the pilot's behalf
Watching the drone with the naked eye and warning the pilot about hazards in the surroundings
Recording the drone's video
questions.explanation: While the pilot flies in goggles, the spotter follows the drone visually and verbally warns the pilot about approaching hazards such as people, vehicles, or obstacles.

questions.prompt: Why do you need a spotter when flying in goggles?
options: Because wearing goggles narrows your field of view and you can't see hazards passing by
Because flying alone is forbidden but not dangerous
Because the drone won't take off without a spotter
Because a spotter extends the battery's life
questions.explanation: Goggles only let you focus on the direction the camera is pointing; you can't see a bird, person, or other aircraft approaching from the side. The spotter fills this gap.

questions.prompt: Which authority regulates drones (UAVs) in Türkiye?
options: TÜBİTAK
SHGM (Directorate General of Civil Aviation)
TRT
Turkish State Meteorological Service
questions.explanation: In Türkiye, drone (UAV) rules are set by SHGM (Directorate General of Civil Aviation); registration is also done through iha.shgm.gov.tr.
